title: Engineering Trading Discipline into Code (Part 1): Structural Discipline in Live Trading
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: HIGH EXTRACT — IConstraint interface pattern, TryOpenOrder() gateway, MaxTradesPerDay/DailyProfitTarget/MaxDailyLoss. Directly implements missing discipline in our execute_signal(). Our system has no daily trade count enforcement.

MaxTradesPerDay.description: Hard stop on trade entries per session

DailyProfitTarget.description: Daily profit target in % of balance — halt trading when reached

MaxDailyLoss.description: Daily loss limit in % of balance — halt trading when breached

iconstraint_interface.IsBreached: Returns True if this constraint is currently violated
iconstraint_interface.ResetIfNewDay: Resets counters when trading day changes (iTime D1 comparison)
iconstraint_interface.UpdateOnTransaction: Updates internal state on each DEAL_ENTRY_IN trade
iconstraint_interface.Reason: Human-readable string explaining breach for logging

try_open_order_gateway.pattern: All trade requests MUST call TryOpenOrder() which checks all constraints before executing
try_open_order_gateway.logic: for constraint in constraints: if constraint.IsBreached(): log(constraint.Reason()); return False
try_open_order_gateway.benefit: Single enforcement point — no bypass possible, no scattered if-statements

daily_reset.method: Compare iTime(_Symbol, PERIOD_D1, 0) to stored dayStartTime
daily_reset.scope: Only DEAL_ENTRY_IN trades count (exits, partials, pending do NOT count)

keep: TryOpenOrder() gateway as single enforcement point
DailyProfitTarget% halts trading when profitable — protect gains
improve: Implement DisciplineEngine class in Python that wraps execute_signal()
Per-symbol trade count (not just global count)
Weekend reset logic for forex (M-F sessions)

creviaderiv_application.missing_in_current: TradingOrchestrator.execute_signal() has no daily count/loss/profit enforcement
creviaderiv_application.implementation: DisciplineEngine.try_execute(signal) checks constraints before MT5 order
